LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Friendly: User Account Control_Switch To The Secure Desktop When Prompting For Elevation
LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Desc: User Account Control: Switch to the secure desktop when prompting for elevation This policy setting controls whether the elevation request prompt is displayed on the interactive user's desktop or the secure desktop. The options are: Enabled: (Default) All elevation requests go to the secure desktop regardless of prompt behavior policy settings for administrators and standard users. Disabled: All elevation requests go to the interactive user's desktop. Prompt behavior policy settings for admin...

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options
GPMapping.Name: User Account Control: Switch to the secure desktop when prompting for elevation

LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Friendly: User Account Control_Type Of Admin Approval Mode
LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Desc: User Account Control: Configure type of Admin Approval Mode. This policy setting controls whether Administrator protection is applied to admin approval mode elevations. If you change this policy setting, you must restart your computer. This policy is only supported on Windows Desktop, not Server. The options are: - Admin Approval Mode is running in legacy mode (default). - Admin Approval Mode is running with Administrator protection.

AllowedValues.2: Admin Approval Mode with Administrator protection.

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options
GPMapping.Name: User Account Control: Configure type of Admin Approval Mode

LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Friendly: User Account Control_Use Admin Approval Mode
LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Desc: User Account Control: Use Admin Approval Mode for the built-in Administrator account This policy setting controls the behavior of Admin Approval Mode for the built-in Administrator account. The options are: Enabled: The built-in Administrator account uses Admin Approval Mode. By default, any operation that requires elevation of privilege will prompt the user to approve the operation. Disabled: (Default) The built-in Administrator account runs all applications with full administrative privilege.

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options
GPMapping.Name: User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account

LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Friendly: User Account Control_Virtualize File And Registry Write Failures To Per User Locations
LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions/UserAccount.Desc: User Account Control: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations This policy setting controls whether application write failures are redirected to defined registry and file system locations. This policy setting mitigates applications that run as administrator and write run-time application data to %ProgramFiles%, %Windir%, %Windir%\system32, or HKLM\Software. The options are: Enabled: (Default) Application write failures are redirected at run time to defined user locati...

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options
GPMapping.Name: User Account Control: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations

LocalUsersAndGroups/Configure.Desc: This Setting allows an administrator to manage local groups on a Device. Possible settings: Update Group Membership: Update a group and add and/or remove members though the 'U' action. When using Update, existing group members that aren't specified in the policy remain untouched. Replace Group Membership: Restrict a group by replacing group membership through the 'R' action. When using Replace, existing group membership is replaced by the list of members specified in the add member section. T...

LockDown/AllowEdgeSwipe.Desc: If you disable this policy setting, users won't be able to invoke any system UI by swiping in from any screen edge. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, users will be able to invoke system UI by swiping in from the screen edges.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow edge swipe
GPMapping.Name: AllowEdgeSwipe

Maps/AllowOfflineMapsDownloadOverMetered.Friendly: Allow Offline Maps Download Over Metered Connection
Maps/AllowOfflineMapsDownloadOverMetered.Desc: Allows the download and update of map data over metered connections. After the policy is applied, you can verify the settings in the user interface in System > Offline Maps.

AllowedValues.0: Disabled. Force disable auto-update over metered connection.
AllowedValues.1: Enabled. Force enable auto-update over metered connection.

Maps/EnableOfflineMapsAutoUpdate.Desc: Disables the automatic download and update of map data. After the policy is applied, you can verify the settings in the user interface in System > Offline Maps.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off Automatic Download and Update of Map Data
GPMapping.Name: TurnOffAutoUpdate

MemoryDump/AllowCrashDump.Desc: This policy setting decides if crash dump collection on the machine is allowed or not. Supported values: 0 - Disable crash dump collection. 1 (default) - Allow crash dump collection.

MemoryDump/AllowLiveDump.Desc: This policy setting decides if live dump collection on the machine is allowed or not. Supported values: 0 - Disable live dump collection. 1 (default) - Allow live dump collection.

Messaging/AllowMessageSync.Desc: This policy setting allows backup and restore of cellular text messages to Microsoft's cloud services.

AllowedValues.1: Message sync is allowed. The user can change this setting.
AllowedValues.0: Message sync isn't allowed and can't be changed by the user.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Message Service Cloud Sync
GPMapping.Name: AllowMessageSync

Messaging/AllowMMS.Desc: This policy setting allows you to enable or disable the sending and receiving cellular MMS messages.

Messaging/AllowRCS.Desc: This policy setting allows you to enable or disable the sending and receiving of cellular RCS (Rich Communication Services) messages.

MixedReality/AADGroupMembershipCacheVali.Friendly: AADGroup Membership Cache Validity In Days
MixedReality/AADGroupMembershipCacheVali.Desc: This policy controls for how many days, Microsoft Entra group membership cache is allowed to be used for Assigned Access configurations targeting Microsoft Entra groups for signed in user. Once this policy is set only then cache is used otherwise not. In order for this policy to take effect, user must sign-out and sign-in with Internet available at least once before the cache can be used for subsequent 'disconnected' sessions.

MixedReality/AllowCaptivePortalBeforeLog.Desc: This policy controls whether the device will display the captive portal flow on the HoloLens sign in screen's network selection page when a captive portal network is detected. Displaying the captive portal flow is disabled by default to reduce the potential of gaining unauthorized access to the device through the browser.

MixedReality/AllowLaunchUriInSingleAppKi.Friendly: Allow Launch Uri In Single App Kiosk
MixedReality/AllowLaunchUriInSingleAppKi.Desc: By default, launching applications via Launcher API is disabled in single app kiosk mode. To enable applications to launch in single app kiosk mode on HoloLens devices, set the policy value to true.

AllowedValues.1: Applications are allowed to be launched with Launcher API, when in single app kiosk mode.
AllowedValues.0: Applications aren't allowed to be launched with Launcher API, when in single app kiosk mode.

MixedReality/AutoLogonUser.Desc: This policy controls whether a user will be automatically logged on. When the policy is set to a non-empty value, it specifies the email address of the auto-logon user. The specified user must logon to the device at least once to enable auto-logon.

MixedReality/AutomaticDisplayAdjustment.Desc: This policy controls if the HoloLens displays will be automatically adjusted for your eyes to improve hologram visual quality when a user wears the device. When this feature is enabled, a new user upon wearing the device won't be prompted to calibrate and yet the displays will be adjusted to suite them automatically. However if an immersive application is launched that depends on eye tracking interactions, the user will be prompted to perform the calibration.

MixedReality/AutoUnlock.Desc: This policy controls whether a signed-in user will be prompted for credentials when returning to the device after the device has entered suspended state. This policy is available both for the device as well as the user scope. When enabled for the device scope, auto unlock will be enabled for all users on the device. When enabled for the user scope, only the specific user will have auto unlock enabled.

AllowedValues.1: User won't be prompted for credentials.
AllowedValues.0: User will be prompted for credentials.

MixedReality/BrightnessButtonDisabled.Desc: This policy setting controls if pressing the brightness button changes the brightness or not. It only impacts brightness on HoloLens and not the functionality of the button when it's used with other buttons as combination for other purposes.

AllowedValues.1: Brightness can't be changed with press of brightness button.
AllowedValues.0: Brightness can be changed with press of brightness button.

MixedReality/ConfigureDeviceStandbyActio.Desc: This policy setting controls device maintenance action during standby.
This policy setting controls when to start maintenance action after device enters standby. The timeout value is in hours.

MixedReality/ConfigureMovingPlatform.Desc: This policy controls the behavior of moving platform feature on HoloLens 2, that is, whether it's turned off / on or it can be toggled by a user. It should only be used by customers who intend to use HoloLens 2 in moving environments with low dynamic motion. Please refer to HoloLens 2 Moving Platform Mode for background information.

AllowedValues.2: Moving platform is enabled and can't be changed by user.
AllowedValues.0: Last set user's preference. Initial state is OFF and after that user's preference is persisted across reboots and is used to initialize the system.
AllowedValues.1: Moving platform is disabled and can't be changed by user.

MixedReality/ConfigureNtpClient.Desc: This policy setting specifies a set of parameters for controlling the Windows NTP Client. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify the following parameters for the Windows NTP Client. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the Windows NTP Client uses the defaults of each of the following parameters. NtpServer. The Domain Name System (DNS) name or IP address of an NTP time source. This value is in the form of "dnsName,flags" where "flags" is a hexadecimal bitmask of t...

MixedReality/ConfigureSharedAccount.Desc: This policy specifies the configuration for Shared Accounts on the device. Shared Accounts are Microsoft Entra accounts that are deployed to the device by an IT admin and can be used by anyone with physical access to the device. These accounts excel in deployments where the HoloLens device is used like a tool shared between multiple people and it doesn't matter which account is used to access Microsoft Entra resources. Because these accounts can be signed in without requiring the user to prov...

MixedReality/DisallowNetworkConnectivity.Desc: Network Connection Status Indicator (NCSI) detects Internet connectivity and corporate network connectivity status. This policy allows IT admins to disable NCSI passive polling. Value type is integer.

MixedReality/EnableStartMenuSingleHandGe.Friendly: Enable Start Menu Single Hand Gesture
MixedReality/EnableStartMenuSingleHandGe.Desc: This policy setting controls if pinching your thumb and index finger, while looking at the Start icon on your wrist, to open the Start menu is enabled or not.

AllowedValues.1: Allow pinching your thumb and index finger, while looking at the Start icon on your wrist, to open the Start menu.
AllowedValues.0: Don't allow pinching your thumb and index finger, while looking at the Start icon on your wrist, to open the Start menu.

MixedReality/EnableStartMenuVoiceCommand.Desc: This policy setting controls if using voice commands to open the Start menu is enabled or not.

AllowedValues.1: Using voice commands to open the Start menu is enabled.
AllowedValues.0: Using voice commands to open the Start menu is disabled.

MixedReality/EnableStartMenuWristTap.Desc: This policy setting controls if tapping the Star icon on your wrist to open the Start menu is enabled or not.

AllowedValues.1: Allow tapping the Start icon on your wrist to open the Start menu.
AllowedValues.0: Don't allow tapping the Start icon on your wrist to open the Start menu.

MixedReality/EyeTrackingCalibrationPromp.Desc: This policy controls when a new person uses HoloLens device, if HoloLens should automatically ask to run eye calibration.

MixedReality/FallbackDiagnostics.Desc: This policy setting controls, when and if diagnostic logs can be collected using specific button combination on HoloLens.

AllowedValues.2: Allowed for all users. Diagnostic logs can be collected by pressing the button combination.
AllowedValues.0: Not allowed. Diagnostic logs can't be collected by pressing the button combination.
AllowedValues.1: Allowed for device owners only. Diagnostics logs can be collected by pressing the button combination only if signed-in user is considered as device owner.

MixedReality/HeadTrackingMode.Desc: This policy configures behavior of HUP to determine, which algorithm to use for head tracking. It requires a reboot for the policy to take effect.

MixedReality/ManualDownDirectionDisabled.Desc: This policy controls whether the user can change down direction manually or not. If no down direction is set by the user, then an automatically calculated down direction is used by the system. This policy has no dependency on ConfigureMovingPlatform policy and they can be set independently.

AllowedValues.1: User isn't allowed to manually change down direction.
AllowedValues.0: User is allowed to manually change down direction.

MixedReality/MicrophoneDisabled.Desc: This policy setting controls whether microphone on HoloLens 2 is disabled or not.

AllowedValues.1: Microphone can't be used for voice.
AllowedValues.0: Microphone can be used for voice.

MixedReality/NtpClientEnabled.Desc: This policy setting specifies whether the Windows NTP Client is enabled. Enabling the Windows NTP Client allows your computer to synchronize its computer clock with other NTP servers. You might want to disable this service if you decide to use a third-party time provider. If you enable this policy setting, you can set the local computer clock to synchronize time with NTP servers. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the local computer clock doesn't synchronize time with NTP ...

MixedReality/PreferLogonAsOtherUser.Desc: This policy configures whether the Sign-In App should prefer showing Other User panel to user.

MixedReality/RequireStartIconHold.Desc: This policy setting controls if it's require that the Start icon to be pressed for 2 seconds to open the Start menu.

AllowedValues.1: Require the Start icon to be pressed for 2 seconds.
AllowedValues.0: Don't require the Start icon to be pressed for 2 seconds.

MixedReality/RequireStartIconVisible.Desc: This policy setting controls if it's required that the Start icon to be looked at when you tap it to open the Start menu.

AllowedValues.1: Require the Start icon to be looked at when you tap it.
AllowedValues.0: Don't require the Start icon to be looked at when you tap it.

MixedReality/SkipCalibrationDuringSetup.Desc: This policy configures whether the device will take the user through the eye tracking calibration process during device setup and first time user setup. If this policy is enabled, the device won't show the eye tracking calibration process during device setup and first time user setup. Note that until the user goes through the calibration process, eye tracking won't work on the device. If an app requires eye tracking and the user hasn't gone through the calibration process, the user will be pr...

AllowedValues.1: Eye tracking calibration process won't be shown during device setup and first time user setup.
AllowedValues.0: Eye tracking calibration process will be shown during device setup and first time user setup.

MixedReality/SkipTrainingDuringSetup.Desc: This policy configures whether the device will take the user through a training process during device setup and first time user setup. If this policy is enabled, the device won't show the training process during device setup and first time user setup. If the user wishes to go through that training process, the user can launch the Tips app.

AllowedValues.1: Training process won't be shown during device setup and first time user setup.
AllowedValues.0: Training process will be shown during device setup and first time user setup.

MixedReality/VisitorAutoLogon.Desc: This policy controls whether a visitor user will be automatically logged in. Visitor users can only be created and logged in, if an Assigned Access profile has been created targeting visitor users. A visitor user will only be automatically logged in, if no other user has logged in on the device before.

AllowedValues.1: Visitor user will be signed in automatically.
AllowedValues.0: Visitor user won't be signed in automatically.

MixedReality/VolumeButtonDisabled.Desc: This policy setting controls if pressing the volume button changes the volume or not. It only impacts volume on HoloLens and not the functionality of the button when it's used with other buttons as combination for other purposes.

AllowedValues.1: Volume can't be changed with press of the volume button.
AllowedValues.0: Volume can be changed with press of the volume button.

MSSecurityGuide/ApplyUACRestrictionsToLo.Friendly: Apply UACRestrictions To Local Accounts On Network Logon

MSSecurityGuide/EnableStructuredExceptio.Friendly: Enable Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection

MSSecurityGuide/TurnOnWindowsDefenderPro.Friendly: Turn On Windows Defender Protection Against Potentially Unwanted Applications

MSSLegacy/AllowICMPRedirectsToOverrideOS.Friendly: Allow ICMPRedirects To Override OSPFGenerated Routes

MSSLegacy/AllowTheComputerToIgnoreNetBIO.Friendly: Allow The Computer To Ignore Net BIOSName Release Requests Except From WINSServers

Multitasking/BrowserAltTabBlowout.Desc: This setting controls the inclusion of app tabs into Alt+Tab. This can be set to show the most recent 3, 5 or 20 tabs, or no tabs from apps. If this is set to show "Open windows only", the whole feature will be disabled.

AllowedValues.3: Open windows and 3 most recent tabs in Microsoft Edge.
AllowedValues.2: Open windows and 5 most recent tabs in Microsoft Edge.
AllowedValues.1: Open windows and all tabs in Microsoft Edge.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure the inclusion of app tabs into Alt-Tab
GPMapping.Name: BrowserAltTabBlowout

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseCloudResource.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. A pipe-separated list of domain cloud resources. Each cloud resource can also be paired optionally with an internal proxy server by using a trailing comma followed by the proxy address. Contains a list of Enterprise resource domains hosted in the cloud. Connections to these resources are considered connections to enterprise networks. If a proxy is paired with a cloud resource, traffic to the cloud resource will be routed through the enterprise netwo...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Enterprise resource domains hosted in the cloud
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_EnterpriseCloudResources

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseInternalProxy.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. A semicolon-separated list of intranet proxy server IP addresses. These addresses are categorized as private by Windows Network Isolation and are accessible to apps that have the Home/Work Networking capability. If you enable this policy setting, it allows an administrator to configure a set of proxies that provide access to intranet resources. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows Network Isolation attempts to discover prox...

GPMapping.Element Name: Type a proxy server IP address for the intranet.
GPMapping.Friendly Name: Intranet proxy servers for  apps
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_Intranet_Proxies

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseIPRange.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. A comma-separated list of IP address ranges that are in your corporate network. If you enable this policy setting, it ensures that apps with the Home/Work Networking capability have appropriate access to your corporate network. These addresses are only accessible to apps if and only if the app has declared the Home/Work Networking capability. Windows Network Isolation attempts to automatically discover private network hosts. By default, the addresse...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Private network ranges for  apps
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_PrivateSubnet

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseIPRangesAreAu.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. Turns off Windows Network Isolation's automatic discovery of private network hosts in the domain corporate environment. If you enable this policy setting, it turns off Windows Network Isolation's automatic discovery of private network hosts in the domain corporate environment. Only network hosts within the address ranges configured via Group Policy will be classified as private. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows Network ...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Subnet definitions are authoritative
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_Authoritative_Subnet

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseNetworkDomain.Desc: This is the list of domains that comprise the boundaries of the enterprise. Data from one of these domains that's sent to a device will be considered enterprise data and protected These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to. This is a comma-separated list of domains, for example contoso. sharepoint. com, Fabrikam. com. Note The client requires domain name to be canonical, otherwise the setting will be rejected by the client. Here are the steps to ...

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseProxyServers.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. A semicolon-separated list of Internet proxy server IP addresses. These addresses are categorized as Internet by Windows Network Isolation and are accessible to apps that have the Internet Client or Internet Client/Server capabilities. If you enable this policy setting, apps on proxied networks can access the Internet without relying on the Private Network capability. However, in most situations Windows Network Isolation will be able to correctly di...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Internet proxy servers for apps
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_Domain_Proxies

NetworkIsolation/EnterpriseProxyServersA.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. Turns off Windows Network Isolation's automatic proxy discovery in the domain corporate environment. If you enable this policy setting, it turns off Windows Network Isolation's automatic proxy discovery in the domain corporate environment. Only proxies configured with Group Policy are authoritative. This applies to both Internet and intranet proxies. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows Network Isolation attempts to automat...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Proxy definitions are authoritative
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_Authoritative_Proxies

NetworkIsolation/NeutralResources.Desc: This setting doesn't apply to desktop apps. A comma-separated list of domain names that can be used as both work or personal resource. For more information see: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/p/?LinkId=234043

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Domains categorized as both work and personal
GPMapping.Name: WF_NetIsolation_NeutralResources

NetworkListManager/AllNetworks_NetworkIc.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify whether users can change the network icon for all networks to which the user connects.

NetworkListManager/AllNetworks_NetworkLo.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify whether users can change the network location for all networks to which the user connects.

NetworkListManager/AllNetworks_NetworkNa.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify whether users can change the network name for all networks to which the user connects.

NetworkListManager/AllowedTlsAuthenticat.Desc: List of URLs (separated by Unicode character 0xF000) to endpoints accessible only within an enterprise's network. If any of the URLs can be resolved over HTTPS, the network would be considered authenticated.

NetworkListManager/ConfiguredTlsAuthenti.Desc: The string will be used to name the network authenticated against one of the endpoints listed in AllowedTlsAuthenticationEndpoints policy.

NetworkListManager/IdentifyingNetworks_L.Desc: This policy setting allows you to configure the Network Location for networks that are in a temporary state while Windows works to identify the network and location type. A network location identifies the type of network that a computer is connected to and automatically sets the appropriate firewall settings for that location.

NetworkListManager/UnidentifiedNetworks_.Desc: This policy setting allows you to configure the Network Location type for networks that Windows can't identify due to a network issue or a lack of identifiable characters in the network information received by the operating system from the network. A network location identifies the type of network that a computer is connected to and automatically sets the appropriate firewall settings for that location.

This policy setting allows you to configure the Network Location user permissions for networks that Windows can't identify due to a network issue or a lack of identifiable characters in the network information received by the operating system from the network.

NewsAndInterests/AllowNewsAndInterests.Desc: This policy specifies whether the widgets feature is allowed on the device. Widgets will be turned on by default unless you change this in your settings. If you turned this feature on before, it will stay on automatically unless you turn it off.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow widgets
GPMapping.Name: AllowNewsAndInterests

NewsAndInterests/DisableWidgetsBoard.Desc: This policy specifies whether to disable the Widgets Board experience. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, you'll be able to invoke the Widgets Board and see its entry point on the taskbar. The Widgets Board experience can be managed in Widgets Settings whose entry-point is located on the Widgets Board. If you enable this policy setting, you won't be able to invoke the Widgets board and its entry point will no longer appear on the taskbar.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable Widgets Board
GPMapping.Name: DisableWidgetsBoard

NewsAndInterests/DisableWidgetsOnLockScr.Desc: This policy specifies whether to disable the Widgets feature on the lock screen. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, widgets will appear on the lock screen and can be managed in the Windows Settings app. If you enable this policy setting, widgets won't appear on the lock screen.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable Widgets On Lock Screen
GPMapping.Name: DisableWidgetsOnLockScreen

Notifications/DisableAccountNotification.Desc: This policy allows you to prevent Windows from displaying notifications to Microsoft account (MSA) and local users in Start (user tile). Notifications include getting users to: reauthenticate; backup their device; manage cloud storage quotas as well as manage their Microsoft 365 or XBOX subscription. If you enable this policy setting, Windows won't send account related notifications for local and MSA users to the user tile in Start. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windo...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off account notifications in Start
GPMapping.Name: DisableAccountNotifications

Notifications/DisallowCloudNotification.Desc: This policy setting blocks applications from using the network to send notifications to update tiles, tile badges, toast, or raw notifications. This policy setting turns off the connection between Windows and the Windows Push Notification Service (WNS). This policy setting also stops applications from being able to poll application services to update tiles. If you enable this policy setting, applications and system features won't be able receive notifications from the network from WNS or via ...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off notifications network usage
GPMapping.Name: NoCloudNotification

Notifications/DisallowNotificationMirror.Desc: This policy setting turns off notification mirroring. If you enable this policy setting, notifications from applications and system won't be mirrored to your other devices. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, notifications will be mirrored, and can be turned off by the administrator or user. No reboots or service restarts are required for this policy setting to take effect.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off notification mirroring
GPMapping.Name: NoNotificationMirroring

Notifications/DisallowTileNotification.Desc: This policy setting turns off tile notifications. If you enable this policy setting, applications and system features won't be able to update their tiles and tile badges in the Start screen. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, tile and badge notifications are enabled and can be turned off by the administrator or user. No reboots or service restarts are required for this policy setting to take effect.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off tile notifications
GPMapping.Name: NoTileNotification

Notifications/EnableExpandedToastNotific.Desc: This policy setting turns on multiple expanded toast notifications in action center. If you enable this policy setting, the first three notifications of each application will be expanded by default in action center. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, only the first notification of each application will be expanded by default in action center. Windows 10 only. This will be immediately deprecated for Windows 11. No reboots or service restarts are required for this policy set...

AllowedValues.1: Enable multiple expanded toasts in action center.
AllowedValues.0: Disable multiple expanded toasts in action center.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn on multiple expanded toast notifications in action center
GPMapping.Name: ExpandedToastNotifications

Notifications/WnsEndpoint.Desc: This policy sets a special WNS FQDN for specific environments.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Enables group policy for the WNS FQDN
GPMapping.Name: WnsEndpoint_Policy

Power/AllowHibernate.Desc: This policy setting decides if hibernate on the machine is allowed or not. Supported values: 0 - Disable hibernate. 1 (default) - Allow hibernate.

Power/AllowStandbyStatesWhenSleepingOnBa.Friendly: Allow Standby States When Sleeping On Battery
Power/AllowStandbyStatesWhenSleepingOnBa.Desc: This policy setting manages whether or not Windows is allowed to use standby states when putting the computer in a sleep state. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows uses standby states to put the computer in a sleep state. If you disable this policy setting, standby states (S1-S3) aren't allowed.

Power/AllowStandbyWhenSleepingPluggedIn.Friendly: Allow Standby When Sleeping Plugged In
Power/AllowStandbyWhenSleepingPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting manages whether or not Windows is allowed to use standby states when putting the computer in a sleep state. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows uses standby states to put the computer in a sleep state. If you disable this policy setting, standby states (S1-S3) aren't allowed.

Power/DisplayOffTimeoutOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify the period of inactivity before Windows turns off the display. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows turns off the display. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting. If the user has configured a slide show to run on the lock screen when the machine is locked, this can prevent the display from turning off. The "Prevent ...

Power/DisplayOffTimeoutPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify the period of inactivity before Windows turns off the display. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows turns off the display. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting. If the user has configured a slide show to run on the lock screen when the machine is locked, this can prevent the display from turning off. The "Prevent ...

Power/EnableEnergySaver.Desc: This policy will extend battery life and reduce energy consumption by enabling Energy Saver to always be on.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Enable Energy Saver to Always Be On
GPMapping.Name: EnableEnergySaver
GPMapping.Path: System > Power Management > Energy Saver Settings

Power/EnergySaverBatteryThresholdOnBatte.Friendly: Energy Saver Battery Threshold On Battery
Power/EnergySaverBatteryThresholdOnBatte.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify battery charge level at which Energy Saver is turned on. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a percent value, indicating the battery charge level. Energy Saver will be automatically turned on at (and below) the specified level. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Energy Saver Battery Threshold (on battery)
GPMapping.Name: EsBattThresholdDC
GPMapping.Path: System > Power Management > Energy Saver Settings

Power/EnergySaverBatteryThresholdPlugged.Friendly: Energy Saver Battery Threshold Plugged In
Power/EnergySaverBatteryThresholdPlugged.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify battery charge level at which Energy Saver is turned on. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a percent value, indicating the battery charge level. Energy Saver will be automatically turned on at (and below) the specified level. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Energy Saver Battery Threshold (plugged in)
GPMapping.Name: EsBattThresholdAC
GPMapping.Path: System > Power Management > Energy Saver Settings

Power/HibernateTimeoutOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify the period of inactivity before Windows transitions the system to hibernate. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows transitions to hibernate. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting. If the user has configured a slide show to run on the lock screen when the machine is locked, this can prevent the sleep transition from ...

Power/HibernateTimeoutPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify the period of inactivity before Windows transitions the system to hibernate. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows transitions to hibernate. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting. If the user has configured a slide show to run on the lock screen when the machine is locked, this can prevent the sleep transition from ...

Power/RequirePasswordWhenComputerWakesOn.Friendly: Require Password When Computer Wakes On Battery
Power/RequirePasswordWhenComputerWakesOn.Desc: This policy setting specifies whether or not the user is prompted for a password when the system resumes from sleep. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, the user is prompted for a password when the system resumes from sleep. If you disable this policy setting, the user isn't prompted for a password when the system resumes from sleep.

Power/RequirePasswordWhenComputerWakesPl.Friendly: Require Password When Computer Wakes Plugged In
Power/RequirePasswordWhenComputerWakesPl.Desc: This policy setting specifies whether or not the user is prompted for a password when the system resumes from sleep. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, the user is prompted for a password when the system resumes from sleep. If you disable this policy setting, the user isn't prompted for a password when the system resumes from sleep.

Power/SelectLidCloseActionOnBattery.Friendly: Select Lid Close Action On Battery
Power/SelectLidCloseActionOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user closes the lid on a mobile PC. Possible actions include: -Take no action -Sleep -Hibernate -Shut down. If you enable this policy setting, you must select the desired action. If you disable this policy setting or don't configure it, users can see and change this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Select the lid switch action (on battery)
GPMapping.Name: DCSystemLidAction_2

Power/SelectLidCloseActionPluggedIn.Friendly: Select Lid Close Action Plugged In
Power/SelectLidCloseActionPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user closes the lid on a mobile PC. Possible actions include: -Take no action -Sleep -Hibernate -Shut down. If you enable this policy setting, you must select the desired action. If you disable this policy setting or don't configure it, users can see and change this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Select the lid switch action (plugged in)
GPMapping.Name: ACSystemLidAction_2

Power/SelectPowerButtonActionOnBattery.Friendly: Select Power Button Action On Battery
Power/SelectPowerButtonActionOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the power button. Possible actions include: -Take no action -Sleep -Hibernate -Shut down. If you enable this policy setting, you must select the desired action. If you disable this policy setting or don't configure it, users can see and change this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Select the Power button action (on battery)
GPMapping.Name: DCPowerButtonAction_2

Power/SelectPowerButtonActionPluggedIn.Friendly: Select Power Button Action Plugged In
Power/SelectPowerButtonActionPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the power button. Possible actions include: -Take no action -Sleep -Hibernate -Shut down. If you enable this policy setting, you must select the desired action. If you disable this policy setting or don't configure it, users can see and change this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Select the Power button action (plugged in)
GPMapping.Name: ACPowerButtonAction_2

Power/SelectSleepButtonActionOnBattery.Friendly: Select Sleep Button Action On Battery
Power/SelectSleepButtonActionOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the sleep button. Possible actions include: -Take no action -Sleep -Hibernate -Shut down. If you enable this policy setting, you must select the desired action. If you disable this policy setting or don't configure it, users can see and change this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Select the Sleep button action (on battery)
GPMapping.Name: DCSleepButtonAction_2

Power/SelectSleepButtonActionPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the sleep button. GPMapping.Friendly Name: Select the Sleep button action (plugged in)
GPMapping.Name: ACSleepButtonAction_2

If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows transitions to sleep. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting. If the user has configured a slide show to run on the lock screen when the machine is locked, this can prevent the sleep transition from occurrin...

If the user has configured a slide show to run on the lock screen when the machine is locked, this can prevent the sleep transition from occurrin...

Power/TurnOffHybridSleepOnBattery.Friendly: Turn Off Hybrid Sleep On Battery
Power/TurnOffHybridSleepOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting allows you to turn off Hybrid Sleep. If you enable this policy setting, a hiberfile isn't generated when the system transitions to sleep (Stand By). If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off hybrid sleep (on battery)
GPMapping.Name: DCStandbyWithHiberfileEnable_2

Power/TurnOffHybridSleepPluggedIn.Friendly: Turn Off Hybrid Sleep Plugged In
Power/TurnOffHybridSleepPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting allows you to turn off Hybrid Sleep. If you enable this policy setting, a hiberfile isn't generated when the system transitions to sleep (Stand By). If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control this setting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off hybrid sleep (plugged in)
GPMapping.Name: ACStandbyWithHiberfileEnable_2

Power/UnattendedSleepTimeoutOnBattery.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify the period of inactivity before Windows transitions to sleep automatically when a user isn't present at the computer. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows automatically transitions to sleep when left unattended. If you specify 0 seconds, Windows doesn't automatically transition to sleep. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control th...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Specify the unattended sleep timeout (on battery)
GPMapping.Name: UnattendedSleepTimeOutDC

Power/UnattendedSleepTimeoutPluggedIn.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify the period of inactivity before Windows transitions to sleep automatically when a user isn't present at the computer. If you enable this policy setting, you must provide a value, in seconds, indicating how much idle time should elapse before Windows automatically transitions to sleep when left unattended. If you specify 0 seconds, Windows doesn't automatically transition to sleep. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users control th...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Specify the unattended sleep timeout (plugged in)
GPMapping.Name: UnattendedSleepTimeOutAC

Printers/ApprovedUsbPrintDevices.Desc: This setting is a component of the Device Control Printing Restrictions. To use this setting, enable Device Control Printing by enabling the "Enable Device Control Printing Restrictions" setting. When Device Control Printing is enabled, the system uses the specified list of vid/pid values to determine if the current USB connected printer is approved for local printing. Type all the approved vid/pid combinations (separated by commas) that correspond to approved USB printer models. When a user ...

Printers/ApprovedUsbPrintDevicesUser.Desc: This setting is a component of the Device Control Printing Restrictions. To use this setting, enable Device Control Printing by enabling the "Enable Device Control Printing Restrictions" setting. When Device Control Printing is enabled, the system uses the specified list of vid/pid values to determine if the current USB connected printer is approved for local printing. Type all the approved vid/pid combinations (separated by commas) that correspond to approved USB printer models. When a user ...

Printers/ConfigureCopyFilesPolicy.Desc: Manages how Queue-specific files are processed during printer installation. At printer installation time, a vendor-supplied installation application can specify a set of files, of any type, to be associated with a particular print queue. The files are downloaded to each client that connects to the print server. You can enable this setting to change the default behavior involving queue-specific files. To use this setting, select one of the options below from the "Manage processing of Queue-spe...

Printers/ConfigureDriverValidationLevel.Desc: This policy setting controls the print driver signature validation mechanism. This policy controls the type of digital signature that's required for a print driver to be considered valid and installed on the system. As part of this validation the catalog/embedded signature is verified and all files in the driver must be a part of the catalog or have their own embedded signature that can be used for validation. You can enable this setting to change the default signature validation method. To u...

Printers/ConfigureIppPageCountsPolicy.Desc: Determines whether to always send page count information for accounting purposes for printers using the Microsoft IPP Class Driver. By default, pages are sent to the printer as soon as they're rendered and page count information isn't sent to the printer unless pages must be reordered. If you enable this setting the system will render all print job pages up front and send the printer the total page count for the print job. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, pages are printed a...

Printers/ConfigureIppTlsCertificatePolic.Desc: Determines the TLS/SSL security policy (WINHTTP_OPTION_SECURITY_FLAGS) for printers using the Microsoft IPP Class Driver. By default, security policy is set to ignore all certificate errors, allowing use of self-signed certificates for printers. If you enable this setting the system defaults to enabling all certificate checking, disallowing certificate errors. Specific certificate checking can be set with the given checkboxes. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, the default is ...

Printers/ConfigureRedirectionGuardPolicy.Desc: Determines whether Redirection Guard is enabled for the print spooler. You can enable this setting to configure the Redirection Guard policy being applied to spooler. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Redirection Guard will default to being 'Enabled'. If you enable this setting you may select the following options: Enabled: Redirection Guard will prevent any file redirections from being followed. Disabled: Redirection Guard won't be enabled and file redirections may be us...

Printers/ConfigureRpcAuthnLevelPrivacyEn.Friendly: Configure Rpc Authn Level Privacy Enabled
Printers/ConfigureRpcAuthnLevelPrivacyEn.Desc: This policy setting controls whether packet level privacy is enabled for RPC for incoming connections. By default packet level privacy is enabled for RPC for incoming connections. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, packet level privacy is enabled for RPC for incoming connections.

Printers/ConfigureRpcConnectionPolicy.Desc: This policy setting controls which protocol and protocol settings to use for outgoing RPC connections to a remote print spooler. By default, RPC over TCP is used and authentication is always enabled. For RPC over named pipes, authentication is always enabled for domain joined machines but disabled for non domain joined machines. Protocol to use for outgoing RPC connections: "RPC over TCP": Use RPC over TCP for outgoing RPC connections to a remote print spooler "RPC over named pipes": Use RPC ...

Printers/ConfigureRpcListenerPolicy.Desc: This policy setting controls which protocols incoming RPC connections to the print spooler are allowed to use. By default, RPC over TCP is enabled and Negotiate is used for the authentication protocol. Protocols to allow for incoming RPC connections: "RPC over named pipes": Incoming RPC connections are only allowed over named pipes "RPC over TCP": Incoming RPC connections are only allowed over TCP (the default option) "RPC over named pipes and TCP": Incoming RPC connections will be allowed ov...

Printers/ConfigureRpcTcpPort.Desc: This policy setting controls which port is used for RPC over TCP for incoming connections to the print spooler and outgoing connections to remote print spoolers. By default dynamic TCP ports are used. RPC over TCP port: The port to use for RPC over TCP. A value of 0 is the default and indicates that dynamic TCP ports will be used. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, dynamic TCP ports are used.

Printers/ConfigureWindowsProtectedPrint.Desc: Determines whether Windows protected print is enabled on this computer. By default, Windows protected print isn't enabled and there aren't any restrictions on the print drivers that can be installed or print functionality. If you enable this setting, the computer will operate in Windows protected print mode which only allows printing to printers that support a subset of inbox Windows print drivers. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, there aren't any restrictions on the print d...

Printers/EnableDeviceControl.Desc: Determines whether Device Control Printing Restrictions are enforced for printing on this computer. By default, there are no restrictions to printing based on connection type or printer Make/Model. If you enable this setting, the computer will restrict printing to printer connections on the corporate network or approved USB-connected printers. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, there are no restrictions to printing based on connection type or printer Make/Model.

Printers/EnableDeviceControlUser.Desc: Determines whether Device Control Printing Restrictions are enforced for printing on this computer. By default, there are no restrictions to printing based on connection type or printer Make/Model. If you enable this setting, the computer will restrict printing to printer connections on the corporate network or approved USB-connected printers. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, there are no restrictions to printing based on connection type or printer Make/Model.

Printers/ManageDriverExclusionList.Desc: This policy setting controls the print driver exclusion list. The exclusion list allows an administrator to curate a list of printer drivers that aren't allowed to be installed on the system. This checks outranks the signature check and allows drivers that have a valid signature level for the Print Driver signature validation policy to be excluded. Entries in the exclusion list consist of a SHA256 hash (or SHA1 hash for Win7) of the INF file and/or main driver DLL file of the driver and the n...

Printers/PointAndPrintRestrictions.Desc: This policy setting controls the client Point and Print behavior, including the security prompts for Windows Vista computers. The policy setting applies only to non-Print Administrator clients, and only to computers that are members of a domain. If you enable this policy setting: -Windows XP and later clients will only download print driver components from a list of explicitly named servers. If a compatible print driver is available on the client, a printer connection will be made. If a compa...

Printers/PointAndPrintRestrictions_User.Desc: This policy setting controls the client Point and Print behavior, including the security prompts for Windows Vista computers. The policy setting applies only to non-Print Administrator clients, and only to computers that are members of a domain. If you enable this policy setting: -Windows XP and later clients will only download print driver components from a list of explicitly named servers. If a compatible print driver is available on the client, a printer connection will be made. If a compa...

Printers/PublishPrinters.Desc: Determines whether the computer's shared printers can be published in Active Directory. If you enable this setting or don't configure it, users can use the "List in directory" option in the Printer's Properties' Sharing tab to publish shared printers in Active Directory. If you disable this setting, this computer's shared printers can't be published in Active Directory, and the "List in directory" option isn't available. Note This settings takes priority over the setting "Automatically publis...

Printers/RequireIppsPolicy.Desc: Determines whether communication with printers using the Microsoft IPP Class Driver must use IPPS (which uses TLS for secure communication). If you enable this policy setting, then only IPP printers which support IPPS can be installed. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, the default is to allow installation of IPP printers which don't support IPPS.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Require IPPS for IPP printers
GPMapping.Name: RequireIppsPolicy

Printers/RestrictDriverInstallationToAdm.Desc: Determines whether users that aren't Administrators can install print drivers on this computer. By default, users that aren't Administrators can't install print drivers on this computer. If you enable this setting or don't configure it, the system will limit installation of print drivers to Administrators of this computer. If you disable this setting, the system won't limit installation of print drivers to this computer.

Privacy/AllowAutoAcceptPairingAndPrivacy.Friendly: Allow Auto Accept Pairing And Privacy Consent Prompts

Privacy/AllowCrossDeviceClipboard.Desc: This policy setting determines whether Clipboard contents can be synchronized across devices. If you enable this policy setting, Clipboard contents are allowed to be synchronized across devices logged in under the same Microsoft account or Microsoft Entra account. If you disable this policy setting, Clipboard contents can't be shared to other devices. Policy change takes effect immediately.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Clipboard synchronization across devices
GPMapping.Name: AllowCrossDeviceClipboard

Privacy/AllowInputPersonalization.Desc: This policy specifies whether users on the device have the option to enable online speech recognition services. If this policy is enabled or not configured, control is deferred to users, and users may choose whether to enable speech services via settings. If this policy is disabled, speech services will be disabled, and users can't enable speech services via settings.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow users to enable online speech recognition services
GPMapping.Name: AllowInputPersonalization
GPMapping.Path: Control Panel > Regional and Language Options

Privacy/DisableAdvertisingId.Desc: This policy setting turns off the advertising ID, preventing apps from using the ID for experiences across apps. If you enable this policy setting, the advertising ID is turned off. Apps can't use the ID for experiences across apps. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users can control whether apps can use the advertising ID for experiences across apps.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off the advertising ID
GPMapping.Name: DisableAdvertisingId

Privacy/DisablePrivacyExperience.Desc: When logging into a new user account for the first time or after an upgrade in some scenarios, that user may be presented with a screen or series of screens that prompts the user to choose privacy settings for their account. Enable this policy to prevent this experience from launching. If this policy is enabled, the privacy experience won't launch for newly created user accounts or for accounts that would've been prompted to choose their privacy settings after an upgrade. If this policy is di...

AllowedValues.1: Don't allow the 'choose privacy settings for your device' screen when a new user logs in or an existing user logs in for the first time after an upgrade.
AllowedValues.0: Allow the 'choose privacy settings for your device' screen for a new user during their first logon or when an existing user logs in for the first time after an upgrade.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Don't launch privacy settings experience on user logon
GPMapping.Name: DisablePrivacyExperience

Privacy/EnableActivityFeed.Desc: This policy setting determines whether ActivityFeed is enabled. If you enable this policy setting, all activity types (as applicable) are allowed to be published and ActivityFeed shall roam these activities across device graph of the user. If you disable this policy setting, activities can't be published and ActivityFeed shall disable cloud sync. Policy change takes effect immediately.

AllowedValues.1: Enabled. Apps/OS can publish the activities and will be roamed across device graph.
AllowedValues.0: Disabled. Apps/OS can't publish the activities and roaming is disabled. (not published to the cloud).

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Enables Activity Feed
GPMapping.Name: EnableActivityFeed

Privacy/LetAppsAccessAccountInfo.Desc: This policy setting specifies whether Windows apps can access account information. You can specify either a default setting for all apps or a per-app setting by specifying a Package Family Name. You can get the Package Family Name for an app by using the Get-AppPackage Windows PowerShell cmdlet. A per-app setting overrides the default setting. If you choose the "User is in control" option, employees in your organization can decide whether Windows apps can access account information by using S...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Let Windows apps access account information
GPMapping.Name: LetAppsAccessAccountInfo

Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Friendly: Let Apps Access Background Spatial Perception
Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Desc: This policy setting specifies whether Windows apps can access the movement of the user's head, hands, motion controllers, and other tracked objects, while the apps are running in the background.

Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Friendly: Let Apps Access Background Spatial Perception_Force Allow These Apps
Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Desc: List of semi-colon delimited Package Family Names of Windows Store Apps. Listed apps are allowed access to the user's movements while the apps are running in the background. This setting overrides the default LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPerception policy setting for the specified apps.

Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Friendly: Let Apps Access Background Spatial Perception_Force Deny These Apps
Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Desc: List of semi-colon delimited Package Family Names of Windows Store Apps. Listed apps are denied access to the user's movements while the apps are running in the background. This setting overrides the default LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPerception policy setting for the specified apps.

Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Friendly: Let Apps Access Background Spatial Perception_User In Control Of These Apps
Privacy/LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPe.Desc: List of semi-colon delimited Package Family Names of Windows Store Apps. The user is able to control the user movements privacy setting for the listed apps. This setting overrides the default LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPerception policy setting for the specified apps.

Privacy/LetAppsActivateWithVoice.Desc: This policy setting specifies whether Windows apps can be activated by voice. If you choose the "User is in control" option, employees in your organization can decide whether Windows apps can be activated with a voice keyword by using Settings > Privacy on the device. If you choose the "Force Allow" option, Windows apps are allowed to be activated with a voice keyword and employees in your organization can't change it. If you choose the "Force Deny" option, Windows apps aren't allowed to be a...

AllowedValues.2: Force deny. Windows apps can't be activated by voice and users can't change it.
AllowedValues.0: User in control. Users can decide if Windows apps can be activated by voice using Settings > Privacy options on the device.
AllowedValues.1: Force allow. Windows apps can be activated by voice and users can't change it.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Let Windows apps activate with voice

If you choose the "Force Allow" option, users can interact with applications using speech while the system is locked and employees in your organization can't change it. If you...

AllowedValues.0: User in control. Users can decide if Windows apps can be activated by voice while the screen is locked using Settings > Privacy options on the device.
AllowedValues.1: Force allow. Windows apps can be activated by voice while the screen is locked, and users can't change it.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Let Windows apps activate with voice while the system is locked
