Start/AlwaysShowNotificationIcon.Desc: This policy setting allows you to show the notification bell icon in the system tray. If you enable this policy setting, the notification icon will always be shown. Otherwise, the notification icon will only be shown when there's a special status (for example, Do Not Disturb is turned on). A reboot is required for this policy setting to take effect.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Show notification bell icon
GPMapping.Name: AlwaysShowNotificationIcon

Start/ConfigureStartPins.Desc: Allows admin to override the default items pinned to Start. This setting lets you specify the default items pinned to Start.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Start Pins
GPMapping.Name: ConfigureStartPins

Start/ConfigureTaskbarSmallButtonBehavio.Desc: This policy setting allows you to configure the behavior of small taskbar buttons. When set to: Always: The taskbar always uses small buttons. When taskbar is full: The taskbar dynamically switches between small buttons and standard button sizes based on available space. Never: The taskbar never uses small buttons. Only standard sized buttons will be used. When this policy is enabled, one of the above options must be selected, and users can't change the "Show smaller taskbar buttons" option i...

AllowedValues.65535: There is no enforced configuration and the setting can be changed by the user.
AllowedValues.2: Never: The taskbar never uses small buttons. Only standard sized buttons will be used. The setting will be grayed out in the Settings UI and the user can't change the setting value.
AllowedValues.0: Always: The taskbar always uses small buttons. The setting will be grayed out in the Settings UI and the user can't change the setting value.
AllowedValues.1: When taskbar is full: The taskbar dynamically switches between small buttons and standard sized buttons based on available space. The setting will be grayed out in the Settings UI and the user can't change the setting value.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Taskbar Small Button Behavior
GPMapping.Name: ConfigureTaskbarSmallButtonBehavior

Start/DisableContextMenus.Desc: This policy allows you to prevent users from being able to open context menus in the Start Menu. If you enable this policy, then invocations of context menus within the Start Menu will be ignored.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable context menus in the Start Menu
GPMapping.Name: DisableContextMenusInStart

Start/DisableControlCenter.Desc: This policy setting removes Quick Settings from the bottom right area on the taskbar. The quick settings area is located at the left of the clock in the taskbar and includes icons for current network and volume. If this setting is enabled, Quick Settings isn't displayed in the quick settings area. A reboot is required for this policy setting to take effect.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Remove Quick Settings
GPMapping.Name: DisableControlCenter

Start/DisableEditingQuickSettings.Desc: If you enable this policy, the user will be unable to modify Quick Settings. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the user will be able to edit Quick Settings, such as pinning or unpinning buttons.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable Editing Quick Settings
GPMapping.Name: DisableEditingQuickSettings

Start/ForceStartSize.Desc: If you enable this policy and set it to Start menu or full screen Start, Start will be that size and users will be unable to change the size of Start in Settings. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows will automatically select the size based on hardware form factor and users will be able to change the size of Start in Settings.

AllowedValues.2: Force a fullscreen size of Start.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Force Start to be either full screen size or menu size
GPMapping.Name: ForceStartSize

Start/HideAppList.Desc: Setting the value of this policy to 1 or 2 collapses the app list. Setting the value of this policy to 3 removes the app list entirely. Setting the value of this policy to 2 or 3 disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

AllowedValues.3: Hide all apps list, remove all apps button, and Disable "Show app list in Start menu" in Settings app.
AllowedValues.2: Hide all apps list, and Disable "Show app list in Start menu" in Settings app.

Start/HideCategoryView.Desc: This policy setting allows you to hide the category view in the Start Menu. If you enable this policy setting, the Start Menu will no longer show the category view as an option and will default to grid view.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Hide Category view in the Start Menu
GPMapping.Name: HideCategoryView

Start/HideChangeAccountSettings.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Change account settings" from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Start/HideFrequentlyUsedApps.Desc: If you enable this setting, the frequently used programs list is removed from the Start menu. If you disable this setting or don't configure it, the frequently used programs list remains on the simple Start menu.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Remove frequent programs list from the Start Menu
GPMapping.Name: NoFrequentUsedPrograms

Start/HideHibernate.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Hibernate" from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Start/HideLock.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Lock" from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Start/HidePeopleBar.Desc: This policy allows you to remove the People Bar from the taskbar and disables the My People experience. If you enable this policy the people icon will be removed from the taskbar, the corresponding settings toggle is removed from the taskbar settings page, and users won't be able to pin people to the taskbar.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Remove the People Bar from the taskbar
GPMapping.Name: HidePeopleBar

Start/HidePowerButton.Desc: Enabling this policy hides the power button from appearing in the start menu.

Start/HideRecentJumplists.Desc: Enabling this policy hides recent jumplists from appearing on the start menu/taskbar and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

Start/HideRecentlyAddedApps.Desc: This policy allows you to prevent the Start Menu from displaying a list of recently installed applications. If you enable this policy, the Start Menu will no longer display the "Recently added" list. The corresponding setting will also be disabled in Settings.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Remove "Recently added" list from Start Menu
GPMapping.Name: HideRecentlyAddedApps

Start/HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites.Desc: Remove Personalized Website Recommendations from the Recommended section in the Start Menu.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Remove Personalized Website Recommendations from the Recommended section in the Start Menu
GPMapping.Name: HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites

Start/HideRecommendedSection.Desc: This policy allows you to prevent the Start Menu from displaying a list of recommended applications and files. If you enable this policy setting, the Start Menu will no longer show the section containing a list of recommended files and apps.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Remove Recommended section from Start Menu
GPMapping.Name: HideRecommendedSection

Start/HideRestart.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Restart/Update and restart" from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Start/HideShutDown.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Shut down/Update and shut down" from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Start/HideSignOut.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Sign out" from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Start/HideSleep.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Sleep" from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

Start/HideSwitchAccount.Desc: Enabling this policy hides "Switch account" from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

Start/HideTaskViewButton.Desc: This policy setting allows you to hide the TaskView button. If you enable this policy setting, the TaskView button will be hidden and the Settings toggle will be disabled.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Hide the TaskView button
GPMapping.Name: HideTaskViewButton

Start/HideUserTile.Desc: Enabling this policy hides the user tile from appearing in the start menu.

Start/ImportEdgeAssets.Desc: This policy setting allows you to import Edge assets to be used with StartLayout policy. Start layout can contain secondary tile from Edge app which looks for Edge local asset file. Edge local asset wouldn't exist and cause Edge secondary tile to appear empty in this case. This policy only gets applied when StartLayout policy is modified.

Start/NoPinningToTaskbar.Desc: This policy setting allows you to control pinning programs to the Taskbar. If you enable this policy setting, users can't change the programs currently pinned to the Taskbar. If any programs are already pinned to the Taskbar, these programs continue to show in the Taskbar. However, users can't unpin these programs already pinned to the Taskbar, and they can't pin new programs to the Taskbar. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, users can change the programs currently pinned ...

Start/ShowOrHideMostUsedApps.Friendly: Show Or Hide Most Used Apps
Start/ShowOrHideMostUsedApps.Desc: If you enable this policy setting, you can configure Start menu to show or hide the list of user's most used apps, regardless of user settings. Selecting "Show" will force the "Most used" list to be shown, and user can't change to hide it using the Settings app. Selecting "Hide" will force the "Most used" list to be hidden, and user can't change to show it using the Settings app. Selecting "Not Configured", or if you disable or don't configure this policy setting, all will allow users to turn...

AllowedValues.2: Force hiding of list of most used apps in Start; corresponding toggle in Setting app is disabled.
AllowedValues.0: Don't enforce visibility of list of most used apps in Start; user can control via Settings app (default behavior equivalent to not configuring this policy).
AllowedValues.1: Force showing of list of most used apps in Start; corresponding toggle in Setting app is disabled.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Show or hide "Most used" list from Start menu
GPMapping.Name: ShowOrHideMostUsedApps

Start/SimplifyQuickSettings.Desc: If you enable this policy, Quick Settings will be reduced to only having the WiFi, Bluetooth, Accessibility, and VPN buttons; the brightness and volume sliders; and battery indicator and link to the Settings app. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the regular Quick Settings layout will appear whenever Quick Settings is invoked.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Simplify Quick Settings Layout
GPMapping.Name: SimplifyQuickSettings

Start/StartLayout.Desc: Specifies the Start layout for users. This setting lets you specify the Start layout for users and prevents them from changing its configuration. The Start layout you specify must be stored in an XML file that was generated by the Export-StartLayout PowerShell cmdlet. To use this setting, you must first manually configure a device's Start layout to the desired look and feel. Once you are done, run the Export-StartLayout PowerShell cmdlet on that same device. The cmdlet will generate an XML fi...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Start Layout
GPMapping.Name: LockedStartLayout

Start/TurnOffAbbreviatedDateTimeFormat.Friendly: Turn Off Abbreviated Date Time Format
Start/TurnOffAbbreviatedDateTimeFormat.Desc: This policy setting allows you to show the longer time and date format in the system tray. If this setting is enabled, the time format will include the AM/PM time marker and the date will include the year. A reboot is required for this policy setting to take effect.

AllowedValues.1: Show classic time and date format.
AllowedValues.0: Show abbreviated time and date format.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off abbreviated time and date format
GPMapping.Name: TurnOffAbbreviatedDateTimeFormat

Stickers/EnableStickers.Desc: This policy setting allows you to control whether you want to allow stickers to be edited and placed on Desktop.

Storage/AllowDiskHealthModelUpdates.Desc: Allows downloading new updates to ML Model parameters for predicting storage disk failure. Enabled: Updates would be downloaded for the Disk Failure Prediction Failure Model. Disabled: Updates wouldn't be downloaded for the Disk Failure Prediction Failure Model. Not configured: Same as Enabled.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow downloading updates to the Disk Failure Prediction Model
GPMapping.Name: SH_AllowDiskHealthModelUpdates

Storage/AllowStorageSenseGlobal.Desc: Storage Sense can automatically clean some of the user's files to free up disk space. By default, Storage Sense is automatically turned on when the machine runs into low disk space and is set to run whenever the machine runs into storage pressure. This cadence can be changed in Storage settings or set with the "Configure Storage Sense cadence" group policy. Enabled: Storage Sense is turned on for the machine, with the default cadence as 'during low free disk space'. Users can't disable Storag...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Storage Sense
GPMapping.Name: SS_AllowStorageSenseGlobal

Storage/AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesC.Friendly: Allow Storage Sense Temporary Files Cleanup
Storage/AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesC.Desc: When Storage Sense runs, it can delete the user's temporary files that aren't in use. If the group policy "Allow Storage Sense" is disabled, then this policy doesn't have any effect. Enabled: Storage Sense will delete the user's temporary files that aren't in use. Users can't disable this setting in Storage settings. Disabled: Storage Sense won't delete the user's temporary files. Users can't enable this setting in Storage settings. Not Configured: By default, Storage Sense will delete the us...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Storage Sense Temporary Files cleanup
GPMapping.Name: SS_AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup

Storage/ConfigStorageSenseCloudContentDe.Friendly: Config Storage Sense Cloud Content Dehydration Threshold
Storage/ConfigStorageSenseCloudContentDe.Desc: When Storage Sense runs, it can dehydrate cloud-backed content that hasn't been opened in a certain amount of days. If the group policy "Allow Storage Sense" is disabled, then this policy doesn't have any effect. Enabled: You must provide the minimum number of days a cloud-backed file can remain unopened before Storage Sense dehydrates it from the sync root. Supported values are: 0 - 365. If you set this value to zero, Storage Sense won't dehydrate any cloud-backed content. The default value ...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Storage Sense Cloud Content dehydration threshold
GPMapping.Name: SS_ConfigStorageSenseCloudContentDehydrationThreshold

Storage/ConfigStorageSenseDownloadsClean.Friendly: Config Storage Sense Downloads Cleanup Threshold
Storage/ConfigStorageSenseDownloadsClean.Desc: When Storage Sense runs, it can delete files in the user's Downloads folder if they haven't been opened for more than a certain number of days. If the group policy "Allow Storage Sense" is disabled, then this policy doesn't have any effect. Enabled: You must provide the minimum number of days a file can remain unopened before Storage Sense deletes it from Downloads folder. Supported values are: 0 - 365. If you set this value to zero, Storage Sense won't delete files in the user's Downloads fo...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Storage Storage Downloads cleanup threshold
GPMapping.Name: SS_ConfigStorageSenseDownloadsCleanupThreshold

Storage/ConfigStorageSenseGlobalCadence.Desc: Storage Sense can automatically clean some of the user's files to free up disk space. If the group policy "Allow Storage Sense" is disabled, then this policy doesn't have any effect. Enabled: You must provide the desired Storage Sense cadence. Supported options are: daily, weekly, monthly, and during low free disk space. The default is 0 (during low free disk space). Disabled or Not Configured: By default, the Storage Sense cadence is set to "during low free disk space". Users can configure t...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Storage Sense cadence
GPMapping.Name: SS_ConfigStorageSenseGlobalCadence

Storage/ConfigStorageSenseRecycleBinClea.Friendly: Config Storage Sense Recycle Bin Cleanup Threshold
Storage/ConfigStorageSenseRecycleBinClea.Desc: When Storage Sense runs, it can delete files in the user's Recycle Bin if they've been there for over a certain amount of days. If the group policy "Allow Storage Sense" is disabled, then this policy doesn't have any effect. Enabled: You must provide the minimum age threshold (in days) of a file in the Recycle Bin before Storage Sense will delete it. Supported values are: 0 - 365. If you set this value to zero, Storage Sense won't delete files in the user's Recycle Bin. The default is 30 days...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Storage Sense Recycle Bin cleanup threshold
GPMapping.Name: SS_ConfigStorageSenseRecycleBinCleanupThreshold

Storage/EnhancedStorageDevices.Desc: This policy setting configures whether or not Windows will activate an Enhanced Storage device. If you enable this policy setting, Windows won't activate unactivated Enhanced Storage devices. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows will activate unactivated Enhanced Storage devices.

Storage/RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess.Desc: This policy setting denies write access to removable disks. If you enable this policy setting, write access is denied to this removable storage class. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, write access is allowed to this removable storage class. Note To require that users write data to BitLocker-protected storage, enable the policy setting "Deny write access to drives not protected by BitLocker," which is located in "Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Com...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Removable Disks: Deny write access
GPMapping.Name: RemovableDisks_DenyWrite_Access_2

Storage/WPDDevicesDenyReadAccessPerDevic.Friendly: WPDDevices Deny Read Access Per Device
Storage/WPDDevicesDenyReadAccessPerDevic.Desc: This policy setting denies read access to removable disks, which may include media players, cellular phones, auxiliary displays, and CE devices. If you enable this policy setting, read access is denied to this removable storage class. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, read access is allowed to this removable storage class.

Storage/WPDDevicesDenyReadAccessPerUser.Friendly: WPDDevices Deny Read Access Per User
Storage/WPDDevicesDenyReadAccessPerUser.Desc: This policy setting denies read access to removable disks, which may include media players, cellular phones, auxiliary displays, and CE devices. If you enable this policy setting, read access is denied to this removable storage class. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, read access is allowed to this removable storage class.

Storage/WPDDevicesDenyWriteAccessPerDevi.Friendly: WPDDevices Deny Write Access Per Device
Storage/WPDDevicesDenyWriteAccessPerDevi.Desc: This policy setting denies write access to removable disks, which may include media players, cellular phones, auxiliary displays, and CE devices. If you enable this policy setting, write access is denied to this removable storage class. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, write access is allowed to this removable storage class.

Storage/WPDDevicesDenyWriteAccessPerUser.Friendly: WPDDevices Deny Write Access Per User
Storage/WPDDevicesDenyWriteAccessPerUser.Desc: This policy setting denies write access to removable disks, which may include media players, cellular phones, auxiliary displays, and CE devices. If you enable this policy setting, write access is denied to this removable storage class. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, write access is allowed to this removable storage class.

Sudo/EnableSudo.Desc: This policy setting controls use of the sudo.exe command line tool. If you enable this policy setting, then you may set a maximum allowed mode to run sudo in. This restricts the ways in which users may interact with command-line applications run with sudo. You may pick one of the following modes to allow sudo to run in: "Disabled": sudo is entirely disabled on this machine. When the user tries to run sudo, sudo will print an error message and exit. "Force new window": When sudo launches a com...

AllowedValues.2: Sudo is allowed in 'disable input' mode.
AllowedValues.1: Sudo is allowed in 'force new window' mode.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure the behavior of the sudo command
GPMapping.Name: EnableSudo

System/AllowBuildPreview.Desc: This policy setting determines whether users can get preview builds of Windows, by configuring controls in Settings > Update and security > Windows Insider Program. If you enable or don't configure this policy setting, users can download and install preview builds of Windows by configuring Windows Insider Program settings. If you disable this policy setting, Windows Insider Program settings will be unavailable to users through the Settings app. This policy is only supported up to Windows 10, ...

AllowedValues.2: Not configured. Users can make their devices available for downloading and installing preview software.
AllowedValues.0: Not allowed. The item "Get Insider builds" is unavailable, users are unable to make their devices available for preview software.
AllowedValues.1: Allowed. Users can make their devices available for downloading and installing preview software.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Toggle user control over Insider builds
GPMapping.Name: AllowBuildPreview
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/AllowCommercialDataPipeline.Desc: This policy is deprecated and will only work on Windows 10 version 1809. Setting this policy will have no effect for other supported versions of Windows. AllowCommercialDataPipeline configures a Microsoft Entra joined device so that Microsoft is the processor of the Windows diagnostic data collected from the device, subject to the Product Terms at https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2185086 To enable this behavior: Enable this policy setting Join a Microsoft Entra account to the device. W...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow commercial data pipeline
GPMapping.Name: AllowCommercialDataPipeline
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/AllowDesktopAnalyticsProcessing.Desc: This policy is deprecated and will only work on Windows 10 version 1809. Setting this policy will have no effect for other supported versions of Windows. This policy setting, in combination with the Allow Telemetry and Configure the Commercial ID, enables organizations to configure the device so that Microsoft is the processor for Windows diagnostic data collected from the device, subject to the Product Terms at https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2185086 To enable this behavior: Enable t...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Desktop Analytics Processing
GPMapping.Name: AllowDesktopAnalyticsProcessing
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/AllowDeviceNameInDiagnosticData.Friendly: Allow Device Name In Diagnostic Data
System/AllowDeviceNameInDiagnosticData.Desc: This policy allows the device name to be sent to Microsoft as part of Windows diagnostic data. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, then device name won't be sent to Microsoft as part of Windows diagnostic data.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow device name to be sent in Windows diagnostic data
GPMapping.Name: AllowDeviceNameInDiagnosticData
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/AllowEmbeddedMode.Desc: Specifies whether set general purpose device to be in embedded mode. Most restricted value is 0.

System/AllowExperimentation.Desc: Note This policy isn't supported in Windows 10, version 1607. This policy setting determines the level that Microsoft can experiment with the product to study user preferences or device behavior. Most restricted value is 0.

AllowedValues.2: Allows Microsoft to conduct full experimentation.
AllowedValues.1: Permits Microsoft to configure device settings only.

System/AllowFontProviders.Desc: This policy setting determines whether Windows is allowed to download fonts and font catalog data from an online font provider. If you enable this policy setting, Windows periodically queries an online font provider to determine whether a new font catalog is available. Windows may also download font data if needed to format or render text. If you disable this policy setting, Windows doesn't connect to an online font provider and only enumerates locally-installed fonts. If you don't configure ...

AllowedValues.1: Allowed. There may be network traffic to fs.microsoft.com and downloadable fonts are available to apps that support them.
AllowedValues.0: Not allowed. No traffic to fs.microsoft.com and only locally installed fonts are available.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Enable Font Providers
GPMapping.Name: EnableFontProviders

System/AllowLocation.Desc: Specifies whether to allow app access to the Location service. Most restricted value is 0. While the policy is set to 0 (Force Location Off) or 2 (Force Location On), any Location service call from an app would trigger the value set by this policy. When switching the policy back from 0 (Force Location Off) or 2 (Force Location On) to 1 (User Control), the app reverts to its original Location service setting. For example, an app's original Location setting is Off. The administrator then sets t...

AllowedValues.2: Force Location On. All Location Privacy settings are toggled on and grayed out. Users can't change the settings and all consent permissions will be automatically suppressed.
AllowedValues.0: Force Location Off. All Location Privacy settings are toggled off and grayed out. Users can't change the settings, and no apps are allowed access to the Location service, including Cortana and Search.
AllowedValues.1: Location service is allowed. The user has control and can change Location Privacy settings on or off.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off location
GPMapping.Name: DisableLocation_2

System/AllowMicrosoftManagedDesktopProce.Desc: This policy is deprecated and will only work on Windows 10 version 1809. Setting this policy will have no effect for other supported versions of Windows. This policy setting configures a Microsoft Entra joined device so that Microsoft is the processor of the Windows diagnostic data collected from the device, subject to the Product Terms at https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2185086 For customers who enroll into the Microsoft Managed Desktop service, enabling this policy is required to al...

System/AllowOOBEUpdates.Desc: This policy allows you to configure whether a new device gets critical updates during the out-of-box experience. If you disable the policy, new devices won't receive critical updates during the out-of-box experience. If you enable the policy, new devices will receive the latest approved critical updates during the out-of-box experience. Note This policy doesn't control the zero-day patch (ZDP) updates page in OOBE. If you have paused quality updates through Windows quality update deferrals an...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Updates in OOBE
GPMapping.Name: AllowOOBEUpdates

System/AllowStorageCard.Desc: Controls whether the user is allowed to use the storage card for device storage. This setting prevents programmatic access to the storage card. Most restricted value is 0.

AllowedValues.0: SD card use isn't allowed and USB drives are disabled. This setting doesn't prevent programmatic access to the storage card.

System/AllowTelemetry.Desc: By configuring this policy setting you can adjust what diagnostic data is collected from Windows. This policy setting also restricts the user from increasing the amount of diagnostic data collection via the Settings app. The diagnostic data collected under this policy impacts the operating system and apps that are considered part of Windows and doesn't apply to any additional apps installed by your organization. Diagnostic data off (not recommended). Using this value, no diagnostic data is se...

AllowedValues.3: Full. All data necessary to identify and help to fix problems, plus data from the Security, Basic, and Enhanced levels.
AllowedValues.0: Security. Information that's required to help keep Windows more secure, including data about the Connected User Experience and Telemetry component settings, the Malicious Software Removal Tool, and Windows Defender.Note: This value is only applicable to Windows 10 Enterprise, Windows 10 Education, Windows 10 Mobile Enterprise, Windows 10 IoT Core (IoT Core), and Windows Server 2016. Using this setting on other devices is equivalent to setting the value of 1.
AllowedValues.1: Basic. Basic device info, including: quality-related data, app compatibility, app usage data, and data from the Security level.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Diagnostic Data
GPMapping.Name: AllowTelemetry
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/AllowUpdateComplianceProcessing.Desc: This policy is deprecated and will only work on Windows 10 version 1809. Setting this policy will have no effect for other supported versions of Windows. This policy setting, in combination with the Allow Telemetry and Configure the Commercial ID, enables organizations to configure the device so that Microsoft is the processor of the Windows diagnostic data collected from the device, subject to the Product Terms at https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2185086 To enable this behavior: Enabl...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow Update Compliance Processing
GPMapping.Name: AllowUpdateComplianceProcessing
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/AllowUserToResetPhone.Desc: Specifies whether to allow the user to factory reset the device by using control panel and hardware key combination. Most restricted value is 0. Tip, This policy is also applicable to Windows 10 and not exclusive to phone.

AllowedValues.1: Allowed to reset to factory default settings.

System/AllowWUfBCloudProcessing.Desc: This policy is deprecated and will only work on Windows 10 version 1809. Setting this policy will have no effect for other supported versions of Windows. This policy setting configures a Microsoft Entra joined device so that Microsoft is the processor of the Windows diagnostic data collected from the device, subject to the Product Terms at https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2185086 To enable this behavior: Enable this policy setting Join a Microsoft Entra account to the device. Set Allow...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow WUfB Cloud Processing
GPMapping.Name: AllowWUfBCloudProcessing
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/BootStartDriverInitialization.Desc: This policy setting allows you to specify which boot-start drivers are initialized based on a classification determined by an Early Launch Antimalware boot-start driver. The Early Launch Antimalware boot-start driver can return the following classifications for each boot-start driver: Good: The driver has been signed and hasn't been tampered with. Bad: The driver has been identified as malware. It's recommended that you don't allow known bad drivers to be initialized. Bad, but required for bo...

System/ConfigureMicrosoft365UploadEndpoi.Desc: This policy sets the upload endpoint for this device's diagnostic data as part of the Desktop Analytics program. If your organization is participating in the program and has been instructed to configure a custom upload endpoint, then use this setting to define that endpoint. The value for this setting will be provided by Microsoft as part of the onboarding process for the program.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure diagnostic data upload endpoint for Desktop Analytics
GPMapping.Name: ConfigureMicrosoft365UploadEndpoint
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/ConfigureTelemetryOptInChangeNoti.Friendly: Configure Telemetry Opt In Change Notification
System/ConfigureTelemetryOptInChangeNoti.Desc: This policy setting controls whether notifications are shown, following a change to diagnostic data opt-in settings, on first logon and when the changes occur in settings. If you set this policy setting to "Disable diagnostic data change notifications", diagnostic data opt-in change notifications won't appear. If you set this policy setting to "Enable diagnostic data change notifications" or don't configure this policy setting, diagnostic data opt-in change notifications appear at first logon...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure diagnostic data opt-in change notifications
GPMapping.Name: ConfigureTelemetryOptInChangeNotification
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx.Friendly: Configure Telemetry Opt In Settings Ux
System/ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx.Desc: This policy setting determines whether an end user can change diagnostic data settings in the Settings app. If you set this policy setting to "Disable diagnostic data opt-in settings", diagnostic data settings are disabled in the Settings app. If you don't configure this policy setting, or you set it to "Enable diagnostic data opt-in settings", end users can change the device diagnostic settings in the Settings app. Note To set a limit on the amount of diagnostic data that's sent to Microsoft...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure diagnostic data opt-in settings user interface
GPMapping.Name: ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/DisableCHPE.Desc: This policy setting controls whether loading CHPE binaries is disabled on the ARM64 device. This policy has no effect on x64 devices. If you enable this policy setting, ARM64 devices won't load CHPE binaries. This setting is required for hotpatching on ARM64 devices. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, ARM64 devices will load CHPE binaries.

System/DisableDeviceDelete.Desc: This policy setting controls whether the Delete diagnostic data button is enabled in Diagnostic & feedback Settings page. If you enable this policy setting, the Delete diagnostic data button will be disabled in Settings page, preventing the deletion of diagnostic data collected by Microsoft from the device. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the Delete diagnostic data button will be enabled in Settings page, which allows people to erase all diagnostic data collected by Mic...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable deleting diagnostic data
GPMapping.Name: DisableDeviceDelete
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/DisableDiagnosticDataViewer.Desc: This policy setting controls whether users can enable and launch the Diagnostic Data Viewer from the Diagnostic & feedback Settings page. If you enable this policy setting, the Diagnostic Data Viewer won't be enabled in Settings page, and it will prevent the viewer from showing diagnostic data collected by Microsoft from the device. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the Diagnostic Data Viewer will be enabled in Settings page.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable diagnostic data viewer
GPMapping.Name: DisableDiagnosticDataViewer
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/DisableDirectXDatabaseUpdate.Desc: This group policy allows control over whether the DirectX Database Updater task will be run on the system.

System/DisableEnterpriseAuthProxy.Desc: This policy setting blocks the Connected User Experience and Telemetry service from automatically using an authenticated proxy to send data back to Microsoft on Windows 10. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, the Connected User Experience and Telemetry service will automatically use an authenticated proxy to send data back to Microsoft. Enabling this policy will block the Connected User Experience and Telemetry service from automatically using an authenticated proxy.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Authenticated Proxy usage for the Connected User Experience and Telemetry service
GPMapping.Name: DisableEnterpriseAuthProxy
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/DisableOneDriveFileSync.Desc: This policy setting lets you prevent apps and features from working with files on OneDrive. If you enable this policy setting: Users can't access OneDrive from the OneDrive app and file picker. Packaged Microsoft Store apps can't access OneDrive using the WinRT API. OneDrive doesn't appear in the navigation pane in File Explorer. OneDrive files aren't kept in sync with the cloud. Users can't automatically upload photos and videos from the camera roll folder. If you disable or don't configure ...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Prevent the usage of OneDrive for file storage
GPMapping.Name: PreventOnedriveFileSync

System/DisableOneSettingsDownloads.Desc: This policy setting controls whether Windows attempts to connect with the OneSettings service. If you enable this policy, Windows won't attempt to connect with the OneSettings Service. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows will periodically attempt to connect with the OneSettings service to download configuration settings.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Disable OneSettings Downloads
GPMapping.Name: DisableOneSettingsDownloads
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/DisableSystemRestore.Desc: Allows you to disable System Restore. This policy setting allows you to turn off System Restore. System Restore enables users, in the event of a problem, to restore their computers to a previous state without losing personal data files. By default, System Restore is turned on for the boot volume. If you enable this policy setting, System Restore is turned off, and the System Restore Wizard can't be accessed. The option to configure System Restore or create a restore point through System Prote...

System/EnableHotpatchAutoRemediation.Desc: This policy setting controls whether Automatic Remediation is enabled on the hotpatch enrolled device. This policy has no effect on devices that doesn't have hotpatch updates installed. If you enable this policy setting, Automatic Remediation is enabled on the hotpatch enrolled device. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Automatic Remediation is disabled on the hotpatch enrolled device.

System/EnableOneSettingsAuditing.Desc: This policy setting controls whether Windows records attempts to connect with the OneSettings service to the EventLog. If you enable this policy, Windows will record attempts to connect with the OneSettings service to the Microsoft\Windows\Privacy-Auditing\Operational EventLog channel. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Windows won't record attempts to connect with the OneSettings service to the EventLog.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Enable OneSettings Auditing
GPMapping.Name: EnableOneSettingsAuditing
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/FeedbackHubAlwaysSaveDiagnosticsL.Friendly: Feedback Hub Always Save Diagnostics Locally
System/FeedbackHubAlwaysSaveDiagnosticsL.Desc: Diagnostic files created when a feedback is filed in the Feedback Hub app will always be saved locally. If this policy isn't present or set to false, users will be presented with the option to save locally. The default is to not save locally.

AllowedValues.1: True. The Feedback Hub should always save a local copy of diagnostics that may be created when a feedback is submitted.
AllowedValues.0: False. The Feedback Hub won't always save a local copy of diagnostics that may be created when a feedback is submitted. The user will have the option to do so.

System/HideUnsupportedHardwareNotificati.Desc: This policy controls messages which are shown when Windows is running on a device that doesn't meet the minimum system requirements for this OS version. If you enable this policy setting, these messages will never appear on desktop or in the Settings app. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, these messages will appear on desktop and in the Settings app when Windows is running on a device that doesn't meet the minimum system requirements for this OS version.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Hide messages when Windows system requirements are not met
GPMapping.Name: HideUnsupportedHardwareNotifications

System/LimitDiagnosticLogCollection.Desc: This policy setting controls whether additional diagnostic logs are collected when more information is needed to troubleshoot a problem on the device. Diagnostic logs are only sent when the device has been configured to send optional diagnostic data. By enabling this policy setting, diagnostic logs won't be collected. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, we may occasionally collect diagnostic logs if the device has been configured to send optional diagnostic data.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Limit Diagnostic Log Collection
GPMapping.Name: LimitDiagnosticLogCollection
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/LimitDumpCollection.Desc: This policy setting limits the type of dumps that can be collected when more information is needed to troubleshoot a problem. Dumps are only sent when the device has been configured to send optional diagnostic data. By enabling this setting, Windows Error Reporting is limited to sending kernel mini dumps and user mode triage dumps. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, we may occasionally collect full or heap dumps if the user has opted to send optional diagnostic data.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Limit Dump Collection
GPMapping.Name: LimitDumpCollection
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindow.Friendly: Limit Enhanced Diagnostic Data Windows Analytics
System/LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindow.Desc: This policy setting, in combination with the "Allow Diagnostic Data" policy setting, enables organizations to send the minimum data required by Desktop Analytics. To enable the behavior described above, complete the following steps: Enable this policy setting Set the "Allow Diagnostic Data" policy to "Send optional diagnostic data". Enable the "Limit Dump Collection" policy Enable the "Limit Diagnostic Log Collection" policy. When these policies are configured, Microsoft will collect only req...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Limit optional diagnostic data for Desktop Analytics
GPMapping.Name: LimitEnhancedDiagnosticDataWindowsAnalytics
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/TelemetryProxy.Desc: With this policy setting, you can forward Connected User Experience and Telemetry requests to a proxy server. If you enable this policy setting, you can specify the FQDN or IP address of the destination device within your organization's network (and optionally a port number, if desired). The connection will be made over a Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) connection. If the named proxy fails, or if you disable or don't configure this policy setting, Connected User Experience and Telemetry data will ...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Connected User Experiences and Telemetry
GPMapping.Name: TelemetryProxy
GPMapping.Path: WindowsComponents > Data Collection and Preview Builds

System/TurnOffFileHistory.Desc: This policy setting allows you to turn off File History. If you enable this policy setting, File History can't be activated to create regular, automatic backups. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, File History can be activated to create regular, automatic backups.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Turn off File History
GPMapping.Name: DisableFileHistory

SystemServices/ConfigureComputerBrowserS.Friendly: Configure Computer Browser Service Startup Mode
SystemServices/ConfigureComputerBrowserS.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services
GPMapping.Name: Computer Browser

SystemServices/ConfigureHomeGroupListene.Friendly: Configure Home Group Listener Service Startup Mode
SystemServices/ConfigureHomeGroupListene.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

GPMapping.Name: HomeGroup Listener

GPMapping.Name: Microsoft FTP Service

SystemServices/ConfigureRemoteProcedureC.Friendly: Configure Remote Procedure Call Locator Service Startup Mode

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services
GPMapping.Name: Special Administration Console Helper

SystemServices/ConfigureSSDPDiscoverySer.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services
GPMapping.Name: SSDP Discovery

SystemServices/ConfigureUPnPDeviceHostSe.Friendly: Configure UPn PDevice Host Service Startup Mode
SystemServices/ConfigureUPnPDeviceHostSe.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services
GPMapping.Name: Web Management Service

SystemServices/ConfigureWindowsMediaPlay.Friendly: Configure Windows Media Player Network Sharing Service Startup Mode
SystemServices/ConfigureWindowsMediaPlay.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

SystemServices/ConfigureXboxLiveAuthMana.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

GPMapping.Path: Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services
GPMapping.Name: Xbox Live Auth Manager

SystemServices/ConfigureXboxLiveGameSave.Friendly: Configure Xbox Live Game Save Service Startup Mode
SystemServices/ConfigureXboxLiveGameSave.Desc: This setting determines whether the service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual.

GPMapping.Name: Xbox Live Networking Service

TaskManager/AllowEndTask.Desc: This setting determines whether non-administrators can use Task Manager to end tasks - enabled (1) or disabled (0). Default: enabled.

AllowedValues.1: Enabled. Users can perform EndTask in TaskManager.
AllowedValues.0: Disabled. EndTask functionality is blocked in TaskManager.

TaskScheduler/EnableXboxGameSaveTask.Desc: This setting determines whether the specific task is enabled (1) or disabled (0).

TenantDefinedTelemetry/CustomTelemetryId.Desc: This policy is used to let mission control what type of Edition we are currently in.

TenantRestrictions/ConfigureTenantRestri.Desc: This setting enables and configures the device-based tenant restrictions feature for Microsoft Entra ID. When you enable this setting, compliant applications will be prevented from accessing disallowed tenants, according to a policy set in your Microsoft Entra tenant. Note Creation of a policy in your home tenant is required, and additional security measures for managed devices are recommended for best protection. Refer to Microsoft Entra tenant Restrictions for more details. https://go.micro...

TextInput/AllowHardwareKeyboardTextSugge.Desc: Placeholder only. Don't use in production environment.

TextInput/AllowIMELogging.Desc: Allows the user to turn on and off the logging for incorrect conversion and saving auto-tuning result to a file and history-based predictive input. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowIMENetworkAccess.Desc: Allows the user to turn on Open Extended Dictionary, Internet search integration, or cloud candidate features to provide input suggestions that don't exist in the device's local dictionary. Most restricted value is 0. In Windows 10, version 1803, we introduced new suggestion services in Japanese IME in addition to cloud suggestion. When AllowIMENetworkAccess is set to 1, all suggestion services are available as predictive input.

TextInput/AllowInputPanel.Desc: Allows the IT admin to disable the touch/handwriting keyboard on Windows. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowJapaneseIMESurrogatePairC.Desc: Allows the Japanese IME surrogate pair characters. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowJapaneseIVSCharacters.Desc: Allows Japanese Ideographic Variation Sequence (IVS) characters. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowJapaneseNonPublishingStan.Friendly: Allow Japanese Non Publishing Standard Glyph
TextInput/AllowJapaneseNonPublishingStan.Desc: Allows the Japanese non-publishing standard glyph. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowJapaneseUserDictionary.Desc: Allows the Japanese user dictionary. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowKeyboardTextSuggestions.Desc: Specifies whether text prediction is enabled or disabled for the on-screen keyboard, touch keyboard, and handwriting recognition tool. When this policy is set to disabled, text prediction is disabled. Most restricted value is 0.

TextInput/AllowLanguageFeaturesUninstall.Desc: When this policy setting is enabled, some language features (such as handwriting recognizers and spell checking dictionaries) included with a language can be uninstalled from a user's machine when the language is uninstalled. The language can be reinstalled with a different selection of included language features if needed. When this policy setting is disabled, language features remain on the user's machine when the language is uninstalled.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Allow uninstallation of language features when a language is uninstalled
GPMapping.Name: AllowLanguageFeaturesUninstall

TextInput/AllowLinguisticDataCollection.Desc: This policy setting controls the ability to send inking and typing data to Microsoft to improve the language recognition and suggestion capabilities of apps and services running on Windows.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Improve inking and typing recognition
GPMapping.Name: AllowLinguisticDataCollection

TextInput/AllowTextInputSuggestionUpdate.Desc: Allows the user to turn on or off the automatic downloading of newer versions of the Expressive Input UI. When downloading isn't allowed the Expressive Input panel will always display the initial UI included with the base Windows image. Most restricted value is 0. The following list shows the supported values: 0 - Not allowed. 1 (default) - Allowed.

TextInput/ConfigureJapaneseIMEVersion.Desc: This policy setting controls the version of Microsoft IME. If you don't configure this policy setting, user can control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is on by default. If you enable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft IME is always selected. If you disable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is always selected. This Policy setting applies only to Microsoft Japanese IME. Note Changes to ...

AllowedValues.2: Does not allow you to configure which Microsoft Japanese IME version to use. The new Microsoft Japanese IME version is always selected.
AllowedValues.0: Allows you to configure which Microsoft Japanese IME version to use. The new Microsoft Japanese IME version is configured by default.
AllowedValues.1: Does not allow you to configure which Microsoft Japanese IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft Japanese IME is always selected.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Japanese IME version
GPMapping.Name: L_ConfigureJapaneseImeVersion

TextInput/ConfigureKoreanIMEVersion.Desc: This policy setting controls the version of Microsoft IME. If you don't configure this policy setting, user can control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is on by default. If you enable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft IME is always selected. If you disable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is always selected. This Policy setting applies only to Microsoft Korean IME. Note Changes to th...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Korean IME version
GPMapping.Name: L_ConfigureKoreanImeVersion

TextInput/ConfigureSimplifiedChineseIMEV.Desc: This policy setting controls the version of Microsoft IME. If you don't configure this policy setting, user can control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is on by default. If you enable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft IME is always selected. If you disable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is always selected. This Policy setting applies only to Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME. Note C...

AllowedValues.2: Does not allow you to configure which Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME version to use. The new Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME version is always selected.
AllowedValues.0: Allows you to configure which Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME version to use. The new Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME version is configured by default.
AllowedValues.1: Does not allow you to configure which Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft Simplified Chinese IME is always selected.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Simplified Chinese IME version
GPMapping.Name: L_ConfigureSimplifiedChineseImeVersion

TextInput/ConfigureTraditionalChineseIME.Desc: This policy setting controls the version of Microsoft IME. If you don't configure this policy setting, user can control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is on by default. If you enable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft IME is always selected. If you disable this, user isn't allowed to control IME version to use. The new Microsoft IME is always selected. This Policy setting applies only to Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME. Note ...

AllowedValues.2: Does not allow you to configure which Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME version to use. The new Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME version is always selected.
AllowedValues.0: Allows you to configure which Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME version to use. The new Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME version is configured by default.
AllowedValues.1: Does not allow you to configure which Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME version to use. The previous version of Microsoft Traditional Chinese IME is always selected.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Traditional Chinese IME version
GPMapping.Name: L_ConfigureTraditionalChineseImeVersion

TextInput/EnableTouchKeyboardAutoInvokeI.Friendly: Enable Touch Keyboard Auto Invoke In Desktop Mode
TextInput/EnableTouchKeyboardAutoInvokeI.Desc: This policy allows the IT admin to control whether the touch keyboard should show up on tapping an edit control. By default, when you tap a textbox, the touch keyboard automatically shows up when there's no keyboard attached. When this policy is enabled, the touch keyboard can be shown or suppressed regardless of the hardware keyboard availability. This policy corresponds to Show the touch keyboard setting in the Settings app.

TextInput/ExcludeJapaneseIMEExceptJIS020.Desc: Allows the users to restrict character code range of conversion by setting the character filter.

AllowedValues.1: All characters except JIS0208 are filtered.

TextInput/ExcludeJapaneseIMEExceptJIS020.Desc: Allows the users to restrict character code range of conversion by setting the character filter.

AllowedValues.1: All characters except JIS0208 and EUDC are filtered.

TextInput/ExcludeJapaneseIMEExceptShiftJ.Desc: Allows the users to restrict character code range of conversion by setting the character filter.

AllowedValues.1: All characters except ShiftJIS are filtered.

TextInput/ForceTouchKeyboardDockedState.Desc: Specifies the touch keyboard is always docked. When this policy is set to enabled, the touch keyboard is always docked.

AllowedValues.2: Touch keyboard docking can be changed.
AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardControllerModeAva.Desc: Specifies whether the controller keyboard mode is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the controller keyboard mode for touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardDictationButtonAv.Desc: Specifies whether the dictation input button is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the dictation input button on touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.2: Dictation button on the keyboard is always disabled.
AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.
AllowedValues.1: Dictation button on the keyboard is always available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardEmojiButtonAvaila.Desc: Specifies whether the emoji button is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the emoji button on touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.2: Emoji button on keyboard is always disabled.
AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.
AllowedValues.1: Emoji button on keyboard is always available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardFullModeAvailabil.Desc: Specifies whether the full keyboard mode is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the full keyboard mode for touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardHandwritingModeAv.Desc: Specifies whether the handwriting input panel is enabled or disabled. When this policy is set to disabled, the handwriting input panel is disabled.

AllowedValues.2: Handwriting input panel is always disabled.
AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.
AllowedValues.1: Handwriting input panel is always available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardNarrowModeAvailab.Desc: Specifies whether the narrow keyboard mode is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the narrow keyboard mode for touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardSplitModeAvailabi.Desc: Specifies whether the split keyboard mode is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the split keyboard mode for touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.

TextInput/TouchKeyboardWideModeAvailabil.Desc: Specifies whether the wide keyboard mode is enabled or disabled for the touch keyboard. When this policy is set to disabled, the wide keyboard mode for touch keyboard is disabled.

AllowedValues.0: The OS determines when it's most appropriate to be available.

TimeLanguageSettings/BlockCleanupOfUnuse.Friendly: Block Cleanup Of Unused Preinstalled Lang Packs
TimeLanguageSettings/BlockCleanupOfUnuse.Desc: This policy setting controls whether the LPRemove task will run to clean up language packs installed on a machine but aren't used by any users on that machine. If you enable this policy setting, language packs that are installed as part of the system image will remain installed even if they aren't used by any user on that system. If you disable or don't configure this policy setting, language packs that are installed as part of the system image but aren't used by any user on that system will ...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Block clean-up of unused language packs
GPMapping.Name: BlockCleanupOfUnusedPreinstalledLangPacks
GPMapping.Path: Control Panel > Regional and Language Options

TimeLanguageSettings/ConfigureTimeZone.Desc: Specifies the time zone to be applied to the device. This is the standard Windows name for the target time zone.

TimeLanguageSettings/MachineUILanguageOv.Desc: This policy setting controls which UI language is used for computers with more than one UI language installed. If you enable this policy setting, the UI language of Windows menus and dialogs for systems with more than one language is restricted to a specified language. If the specified language isn't installed on the target computer or you disable this policy setting, the language selection defaults to the language selected by the local administrator. If you disable or don't configure this po...

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Force selected system UI language to overwrite the user UI language
GPMapping.Name: MachineUILanguageOverwrite
GPMapping.Path: Control Panel > Regional and Language Options

TimeLanguageSettings/RestrictLanguagePac.Friendly: Restrict Language Packs And Features Install
TimeLanguageSettings/RestrictLanguagePac.Desc: This policy setting restricts the install of language packs and language features, such as spell checkers, on a device.

Troubleshooting/AllowRecommendations.Desc: This policy setting configures how troubleshooting for known problems can be applied on the device and lets administrators configure how it's applied to their domains/IT environments. Not configuring this policy setting will allow the user to configure how troubleshooting is applied. Enabling this policy allows you to configure how troubleshooting is applied on the user's device. You can select from one of the following values: 0 = Don't allow users, system features, or Microsoft to apply tro...

AllowedValues.2: Prompt - Automatically apply troubleshooting for critical problems detected by system features and Microsoft. Prompt users when troubleshooting for other problems is available and allow the user to choose to apply or ignore.
AllowedValues.5: Configurable - Allow the user to choose their own troubleshooting settings.
AllowedValues.3: Notify - Automatically apply troubleshooting for critical and other problems detected by system features and Microsoft. Notify users when troubleshooting has solved a problem.
AllowedValues.4: Silent - Automatically apply troubleshooting for critical and other problems detected by system features and Microsoft. Don't notify users when troubleshooting has solved a problem.
AllowedValues.1: Critical - Automatically apply troubleshooting for critical problems detected by system features and Microsoft. Don't notify users when troubleshooting has solved a problem.
AllowedValues.0: Off - Don't allow users, system features, or Microsoft to apply troubleshooting.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Troubleshooting: Allow users to access recommended troubleshooting for known problems
GPMapping.Name: TroubleshootingAllowRecommendations
GPMapping.Path: System > Troubleshooting and Diagnostics > Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool

Update/MaintenanceWindowDurationHours.Desc: Configure the duration of the maintenance window in hours. Minimum value is 2, maximum value is 24.

Update/MaintenanceWindowEnabled.Desc: Specifies whether maintenance window is enabled. This policy and its associated policies are currently in public preview.

Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlyMonthBase.Friendly: Maintenance Window Monthly Month Based Day Of Month
Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlyMonthBase.Desc: If the maintenance window repeat schedule is set to monthly, and set to repeat on the same date each month, configure the day of the month to repeat maintenance window occurrence (for example, 2 = repeat on the 2nd day of the month). Maximum value is 28.

Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlySchedule.Desc: If the maintenance window repeat schedule is set to monthly, configure whether to repeat on the same date each month (the 1st of every month), a day within a specific week (for example, the second Tuesday of every month), or on the last day of each month. 1=repeat on same date each month, 2=repeat on a day of a specific week, 3=repeat on the last day of each month.

Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlyWeekBased.Friendly: Maintenance Window Monthly Week Based Day Of The Week
Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlyWeekBased.Desc: If the maintenance window repeat schedule is set to monthly, and set to repeat on the a specific day of a specific week, configure the specific day of the week to repeat maintenance window occurrence: 1=Sunday, 2=Monday, 3=Tuesday, 4=Wednesday, 5=Thursday, 6=Friday, 7=Saturday.

Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlyWeekBased.Friendly: Maintenance Window Monthly Week Based Occurrence In Month
Update/MaintenanceWindowMonthlyWeekBased.Desc: If the maintenance window repeat schedule is set to monthly, and set to repeat on the a specific day of a specific week, configure the specific week to repeat maintenance window occurrence. 1=first week, 2=second week, 3=third week, 4=fourth week, 5=last week.

Update/MaintenanceWindowRepeatScheduleOp.Desc: Configure whether and how often the maintenance window repeats, 1=no repeat, 2=daily, 3=weekly, 4=monthly. During preview, daily/weekly/monthly schedule will be repeated every day/week/month.

Update/MaintenanceWindowStartDate.Desc: Configure the start date for the maintenance window in the format of YYYY-MM-DD (for example, 2025-12-16). During public preview, this field will only be honored for a non-repeating maintenance window. Repeating maintenance windows will start on the earliest date that meets the configured schedule.

Update/MaintenanceWindowStartTime.Desc: Configure the start time for the maintenance window in HH:MM format (for example, 23:30).

Update/MaintenanceWindowUpdateActions.Desc: Specifies which update operations are allowed only during the maintenance window, 1 = Download, install and restart, 2 = Install and restart, 3 = Restart only.

Update/MaintenanceWindowWeeklyFriday.Desc: If the maintenance window repeat schedule is set to weekly, configure whether Friday is included in the weekly schedule.

Update/MaintenanceWindowWeeklyMonday.Desc: SIf the maintenance window repeat schedule is set to weekly, configure whether Monday is included in the weekly schedule.

Update/AllowAutoUpdate.Desc: Enables the IT admin to manage automatic update behavior to scan, download, and install updates. Important. This option should be used only for systems under regulatory compliance, as you won't get security updates as well. If the policy isn't configured, end-users get the default behavior (Auto install and restart).

AllowedValues.2: Auto install and restart. Updates are downloaded automatically on non-metered networks and installed during "Automatic Maintenance" when the device isn't in use and isn't running on battery power. If automatic maintenance is unable to install updates for two days, Windows Update will install updates right away. If a restart is required, then the device is automatically restarted when the device isn't actively being used. This is the default behavior for unmanaged devices. Devices are updated quickly, but it increases the risk of accidental data loss caused by an application that doesn't shutdown properly on restart.
AllowedValues.3: Auto install and restart at a specified time. The IT specifies the installation day and time. If no day and time are specified, the default is 3 AM daily. Automatic installation happens at this time and device restart happens after a 15-minute countdown. If the user is logged in when Windows is ready to restart, the user can interrupt the 15-minute countdown to delay the restart.
AllowedValues.4: Auto install and restart without end-user control. Updates are downloaded automatically on non-metered networks and installed during "Automatic Maintenance" when the device isn't in use and isn't running on battery power. If automatic maintenance is unable to install updates for two days, Windows Update will install updates right away. If a restart is required, then the device is automatically restarted when the device isn't actively being used. This setting option also sets the end-user control panel to read-only.
AllowedValues.1: Auto install the update and then notify the user to schedule a device restart. Updates are downloaded automatically on non-metered networks and installed during "Automatic Maintenance" when the device isn't in use and isn't running on battery power. If automatic maintenance is unable to install updates for two days, Windows Update will install updates immediately. If the installation requires a restart, the end-user is prompted to schedule the restart time. After the update is installed, if the user hasn't scheduled a restart, the device will attempt to restart automatically. The user will be notified about the scheduled restart and can reschedule it if the proposed time is inconvenient. Enabling the end-user to control the start time reduces the risk of accidental data loss caused by applications that don't shutdown properly on restart.
AllowedValues.0: Notify the user before downloading the update. This policy is used by the enterprise who wants to enable the end-users to manage data usage. With this option users are notified when there are updates that apply to the device and are ready for download. Users can download and install the updates from the Windows Update control panel.

GPMapping.Friendly Name: Configure Automatic Updates
GPMapping.Name: AutoUpdateCfg
GPMapping.Path: Windows Components > Windows Update > Manage end user experience
